##NAMES=Naalasite
##RRUFFID=R250033
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=NaAl(AsO_3_OH)_2_·H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Tightly intergrown aggregates and druses of equant light to medium pink, transparent crystals associated with anhydrite, juansilvaite, magnesiokoritnigite. Type sample described in Kampf et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250033
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Na_0.92_Al_0.61_Fe^3+^_0.39_As_2_O_9_H_4.07_ ; Crystals exhibit significant mosaicity, and variable Fe/Al composition
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, profile is calculated with Cu radiation
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.494 b: 8.494 c: 26.43 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 1651.5 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
